tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All fail-to-pass tests execute Python subprocesses that import the module, create actual httpx.AsyncClient instances, and inspect runtime properties (`_trust_env`, `_mounts`, `isinstance` checks). The syntax check uses `ast.parse`. No test is purely text-matching on source code.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests would fail on the original code because `httpx.AsyncClient()` defaults to `trust_env=True`, which causes `_trust_env` to be True and `_mounts` to contain proxy transports when HTTP_PROXY is set. A stub or no-op would not pass these assertions. The tests explicitly set proxy env vars before importing and assert the opposite of default behavior.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction requires both clients (init_http_client and _HttpPosterActor) to communicate without system proxy. Tests cover both: `test_init_client_trust_env_disabled` and `test_init_client_proxy_not_mounted` for the first, `test_ray_actor_trust_env_disabled` for the second. Additional tests verify preserved functionality (connection limits, timeout).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass categories), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining what each test verifies.

structured_data_schema.reason: The task does not produce structured data (JSON/CSV/API/DB); it modifies Python source code to change httpx client behavior.
